scope_description: 5-generation Minsk/Brest Jewish family reconstructed from 1834, 1850 and 1858 revision lists with missing female enumeration, age drift, registered-vs-actual residence, household-number collisions and false parent-child assignments.

input_dna_matches.expected_link: surname-variant clue only; insufficient to merge I5/I8
small endogamous/collateral clue; not father proof

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Household 44: Leib Raskes age 24; son Nakhman age 2. Registered in Brest Jewish community.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: anchors I9 -> I5 household relationship; ages approximate.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Household 44: Leib Raskes age 39; son Nakhman age 14; note: absent in actual residence, registered here.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: shows age drift and registered-vs-actual residence; supports continuity from 1834.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Household 44: Nakhman Raskes age 19, son Yankel age 3. Wife not enumerated in this copy. Female household members absent from summary page.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports I5 -> I3; absence of wife Sura is not proof she did not exist.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Marriage of Nakhman Raskes, son of Leib, to Sura Friedman, daughter of Gersh.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms wife Sura and correct parentage; resolves female absence in revision list.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Yankel, son of Nakhman Raskes and Sura Friedman.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms I3 parents; resolves age/household interpretation.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Household 91: Yankel Raskes age 20, son of Movsha, registered in Kobryn.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: separates I7 from Brest household; not father of I3.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Yankel Raskes of Kobryn merged with Brest Yankel; Chaya added as wife of Leib because female missing in 1834 revision list.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: should be flagged as same-name merge and invented female bridge.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Leib's wife was Chaya because some trees say so.
Yankel Raskes from Kobryn is the same as Brest Yankel because name and age are similar.
Sura Friedman may not be mother because absent in 1858 revision list.
input_user_assertions.evidence: not enumerated in revision list household copy

embedded_errors.reason: wife not enumerated in summary page; metric records confirm her
Brest household vs Kobryn household; different father
public tree invents Chaya without source
age differences 1834/1850/1858 are within revision-list drift range
1850 says registered in Brest but absent/actual residence elsewhere
Raskes/Raskin and low DNA are insufficient without household continuity
public tree converts revision-list gaps into invented relationships

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse revision-list household numbers, names, ages, relationship terms and registered-residence notes.
Step 2: Correlate household 44 across 1834, 1850 and 1858; allow reasonable age drift.
Step 3: Identify I9 Leib -> I5 Nakhman continuity from household records.
Step 4: Identify I5 Nakhman -> I3 Yankel from 1858 household plus metric birth src_1705.
Step 5: Treat missing female enumeration in 1858 as negative-space caveat, not disproof of Sura Friedman.
Step 6: Use marriage src_1704 and birth src_1705 to confirm Sura Friedman as wife/mother.
Step 7: Separate I7 Kobryn Yankel from I3 Brest Yankel using household, father and place differences.
Step 8: Reject invented Chaya wife profile unless future source appears; keep I10 as unknown wife placeholder only if needed.
Step 9: Treat Raskes/Raskin Pruzhany match as surname-variant hypothesis, not merge.
Step 10: Output household-linked reconstructed tree with evidence status and flagged revision-list traps.

identity_I5_I8_same_person.reason: surname variant and weak DNA only; no household continuity

spouse_I10_named_Chaya.reason: invented from public tree; no source

female_absence_disproves_Sura.reason: female absence in revision list is not disproof

true_lineage: I3 Yankel Nakhmanovich Raskes + I4 Freida Raskes
I5 Nakhman Raskes + I6 Sura Friedman
false_claims: Sura Friedman is not mother because absent from the 1858 revision list.
Kobryn Yankel Raskes is same as Brest Yankel Raskes.
Raskes and Raskin candidate profiles can be merged on surname similarity and weak DNA.

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must confirm Sura Friedman as mother despite missing female enumeration.
Engine must not invent Chaya as named wife.
Engine must allow revision-list age drift and preserve household continuity.
Engine must classify Raskes/Raskin as hypothesis/conflict, not merge.

created_for: AutoTreeGen Test Tree Corpus + Evaluation Suite
